inbound.context: - [ADR-0000: Core Premise](0000-core-premise.md)
Trails implementations never throw. That's a hard rule — [ADR-0000](0000-core-premise.md) established it. Input in, `Result` out. The warden enforces it. If an implementation throws, it's a bug.
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](0000-core-premise.md) — the foundational decisions, particularly "the trail is the product" and "derive by default"

- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](0000-core-premise.md) — the foundational decisions this pipeline serves, especially "implementations are pure" and "trailheads are peers"
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](0000-core-premise.md) — the foundational decisions; warden is the governance arm of "author, derive, declare — guard against drift"
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](0000-core-premise.md) — "derive by default, override deliberately" principle
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](0000-core-premise.md) — "the trail is the product," "derive by default," and the information architecture
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](0000-core-premise.md) — "the trail is the product," "derive by default," and the information architecture that infrastructure trails inherit

- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](../0000-core-premise.md) -- "author what's new, derive what's known, override what's wrong"; provisions leverage derived contract data for upgrades
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](../0000-core-premise.md) -- "the trail is the product"; packs group trails into capability boundaries
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](../0000-core-premise.md) — "derive by default"; fires declare activation, the framework derives the reactive graph
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](../0000-core-premise.md) — "derive by default"; framework lifecycle events are derived from execution observation
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](../0000-core-premise.md) -- "derive by default, override deliberately"; the information architecture categories

- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](../0000-core-premise.md) -- "trailheads are peers"; WebSocket is the fourth trailhead, following the same patterns as CLI, MCP, and HTTP
- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](../0000-core-premise.md): "define once, trailhead everywhere" and the information architecture categories (authored, projected, overridden)

- [ADR-0000: Core Premise](../0000-core-premise.md) -- the trail is the product, everything else is a projection
